# @dpup/meshcore-sim

> A deterministic, behavioral MeshCore network simulator for testing
> meshcore.js / @dpup/meshcore-ts apps without radios. Models observable mesh
> behavior (message arrival, ordering, loss, signal metadata, node state,
> channel/decrypt outcomes) — not RF physics. ESM-only, Node ≥ 18. A
> development/test dependency, never shipped in production.

The core integration: `SimConnection` implements the raw `@liamcottle/meshcore.js`
`Connection` surface and is injected into `@dpup/meshcore-ts`'s `MeshCoreClient`
via `new MeshCoreClient(sim.asConnection(), options)`. A `SimClock` gives the
test full control over time (`advance`/`runUntil`/`runAll`), so 30-second
debounce windows run instantly and deterministically. Worlds are authored with
`defineWorld` and its builders (`node`/`channel`/`contact`) or generated
procedurally with `generateWorld`. Scenarios (dynamic timelines) are built with
`scenario`/`at` or the `traffic` generators (`burst`, `crosstalk`, `quiet`,
`outOfOrder`). Both worlds and scenarios can be frozen to committed JSON fixtures
with `serializeWorld`/`loadWorld` and `serializeScenario`/`loadScenario`.

The provenance model is the headline capability: a `ChannelMessageEvent` with
`verified: false` surfaces only as `channelData` (raw bytes), never as a decoded
`channelMessage` — the admin-gate adversarial input you cannot safely produce on
real hardware.

## Docs

- [API reference](https://github.com/dpup/meshcore-sim/blob/main/docs/api.md): Complete, generated reference. Source of truth for all signatures, types, enums, and errors.
- [Guide](https://github.com/dpup/meshcore-sim/blob/main/docs/guide.md): Concepts and recipes — worlds, the Clock, scenarios & provenance, generators, freezing fixtures, testing patterns, and integrating with MeshCoreClient.
- [README](https://github.com/dpup/meshcore-sim/blob/main/README.md): Overview, install, quickstart, and design philosophy.

## Examples

- [demo](https://github.com/dpup/meshcore-sim/blob/main/examples/demo.ts): A guided tour — build a world, connect a real MeshCoreClient, static reads, dynamic traffic in virtual time, provenance (verified vs. unverified), determinism, and freeze/reload. Run with: `bun examples/demo.ts [--seed <n>]`.

## Notes

- [AGENTS.md](https://github.com/dpup/meshcore-sim/blob/main/AGENTS.md): Architecture, the Connection contract, the provenance mapping table, the full layout (including `duration.ts`, `keys.ts`, `traffic.ts`, `generate.ts`, `serialize.ts`), conventions, and the don't-regress list.
- The reference (docs/api.md) is generated by `bun run docs` and CI-verified to stay in sync with the source (`bun run docs:check`).
